# Test Fixtures — Ground Truth

Generated by `scripts/make-fixtures.py`. Do not edit by hand.
These recorded values are the ground truth the core-IO tests assert against.

## tiny.las / tiny.laz

Same georeferenced point cloud, written in both LAS and LAZ.
LAS point format 6, version 1.4.

- pointCount: 12
- versionMinor: 4
- scale: [0.001000, 0.001000, 0.001000]
- offset: [500000.000000, 4100000.000000, 200.000000]
- firstPoint: [500123.456000, 4100876.789000, 210.250000]
- min: [500123.456000, 4100876.789000, 210.250000]
- max: [500134.500000, 4100887.500000, 215.000000]

## tiny.ply

Local-coordinate point cloud with per-vertex RGB color.

- pointCount: 10
- firstPoint: [0.000000, 0.000000, 0.000000]
- min: [0.000000, 0.000000, 0.000000]
- max: [9.000000, 4.500000, 2.250000]

## tiny.obj

Small local-coordinate mesh (a unit-ish cube).

- vertexCount: 8
- firstVertex: [0.000000, 0.000000, 0.000000]
- min: [0.000000, 0.000000, 0.000000]
- max: [2.000000, 2.000000, 2.000000]

## tiny.glb

Same cube mesh as tiny.obj, exported as binary glTF.

- vertexCount: 8
- firstVertex: [0.000000, 0.000000, 0.000000]
- min: [0.000000, 0.000000, 0.000000]
- max: [2.000000, 2.000000, 2.000000]
